name: Iterative Extension
purpose: Support repeated, incremental extension of verified stable structures across multiple runs of the framework. New structures are built from XNOR-stable inputs and remain easy to re-ingest in future passes.
design_assumptions: The user will typically rerun the lens multiple times on the same or evolving set of structures.
Growth happens incrementally across sessions rather than in a single pass.
Automation is maximized for low-risk operations on stable inputs.
Traceability must remain usable across multiple runs.

conditions: Input must consist of XNOR-stable invariants or previously verified Stage 7 structures.
Session-level authorization from the human operator is sufficient for the run.

rules: Pure Overlap on stable inputs runs automatically.
Basic consistency verification of new structures runs automatically.
Scope changes are recorded automatically but flagged if they significantly alter domain.
Deeper compositions (depth 4+) are allowed but logged with higher visibility for later review.

operations.name: Overlap
operations.implementation: Executed via AND gate on stable truth vectors. Fully automated.
operations.notes: Primary growth mechanism. Safe for repeated use.

operations.name: Scope
operations.implementation: Boundary defined as metadata. Can be added automatically but should be reviewed when domain changes meaningfully.
operations.notes: Use sparingly across multiple runs to avoid accumulating complex boundaries.

operations.name: Sequence
operations.implementation: Structural metadata only. Does not use logic gates.
operations.notes: Useful for organizing structures across multiple passes.

features: Previous Stage 7 outputs can be re-ingested directly as stable inputs in later runs.
Each new structure carries a lightweight version or iteration marker for tracking across runs.
Automated logging includes parent structures and operations used, enabling clean continuation.
No forced human approval between runs unless Scope or high-depth changes occur.

verification.description: Automated consistency check is performed on every new structure. Failures are logged and can be reviewed in subsequent runs.

constraints: Only XNOR-stable inputs may be used.
No new premises may be introduced.
Automation applies to recombination and verification, not to final external use of structures.
Human retains full ability to review or override at any point across runs.
